rect.data-narration: Packages are a collection of related classes, interfaces, and other types that are used together.

tspan.data-narration: In object-oriented programming (OOP), a package is a collection of related classes, interfaces, and other types that are used together.

rect.data-narration: A package structure typically consists of the following components: package declaration, class declaration, interface declaration, and import statements.

tspan.data-narration: A package structure typically consists of the following components: package declaration, class declaration, interface declaration, and import statements.

rect.data-narration: Importing packages allows you to use types from another package in your code. The import keyword is used to import packages.

tspan.data-narration: Importing packages allows you to use types from another package in your code. The import keyword is used to import packages.

rect.data-narration: Package members include classes, interfaces, and other types that are used together.

tspan.data-narration: Package members include classes, interfaces, and other types that are used together.

rect.data-narration: Access modifiers determine the accessibility of package members.

tspan.data-narration: Access modifiers determine the accessibility of package members.

rect.data-narration: Best practices for using packages include organizing code into logical groups, using descriptive package names, and avoiding unnecessary imports.

tspan.data-narration: Best practices for using packages include organizing code into logical groups, using descriptive package names, and avoiding unnecessary imports.
By following these guidelines, you can effectively use packages to organize and structure your code.
